However, I can provide a paragraph about the Heliotropium plant species as it shares a similar name with it ( Heliotropium species also include plants like Heliotropium arborescens and Heliotropium curassavicum that have been known to be called 'Helopus villosus'). 

The Heliotropium plant, specifically Heliotropium arborescens, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Heliotropium arborescens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Heliotropium arborescens include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote well-being.
